behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: CSV parser must preserve row index, validator must reject, and DLQ writer must persist both payload and original row number. This is beyond a single pure function (MOCK-MISS), but does not require real OS/network/subprocess if DLQ is an in-process test double (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a/b/c all no). Rejected higher level: behavior text does not require real S3/SQS transport, only that rejected rows appear in DLQ with row number.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser + schema validator + transform gate + DLQ adapter in one in-process test. Feed a CSV fixture with known line numbers and missing required fields; assert each invalid row is absent from success writes and appears in DLQ with the exact original row number and raw row content.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is transformation logic in one boundary: parse/normalize date formats to canonical output. No required module interaction, infra, or model output dependency (MOCK-MISS indicates single-function logic). Rejected higher level: behavior text is strictly about conversion correctness, not pipeline wiring or I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test for date normalization function with ISO 8601, US, and European inputs (plus invalid/ambiguous cases), asserting exact canonical output string or explicit validation error.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real runtime behavior: memory pressure, GC, wall-clock timeout, and process limits under large input (REAL-INFRA yes). In-process mocks can hide OOM/timeout regressions (MOCK-HIDE yes). No LLM dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline as a real process against a generated 500k-row CSV and real storage targets used in deployment-like config. Assert completion before configured timeout and peak RSS stays below budget (or no OOM/kill), with expected row counts.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets real HTTP error semantics from S3 (503) and retry/backoff behavior; SDK/network interaction can differ from mocks (REAL-INFRA yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Integration: text specifies S3 transient 503 handling, which is an external HTTP boundary where stubbed clients can miss real failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Execute pipeline against a controllable S3-compatible endpoint/test server that returns 503 for first N requests then 200. Verify retry attempts and exponential backoff timing envelope, and assert batch eventually succeeds without full-batch failure.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Requires durable DB state plus real process restart semantics (subprocess lifecycle), so failure boundary is system-level. Integration with function-level retries may miss restart/idempotency bugs (REAL-INFRA yes via process + DB behavior). No LLM dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Start pipeline process on a batch, force termination mid-run after partial commits, then restart with same input and checkpoint state. Assert final PostgreSQL table has no duplicates (by business key/unique constraint) and exactly-once row counts.
